why_line: Already scored 06-11 as TC-2026-0612-002 — but listed here only to note the dedup; not re-approved.
Musk's SpaceX closed its debut up 19% to a ~$1.96T valuation, making him the first trillionaire on paper — the IPO that priced last week consummated and minted the number.
Washington forced Amodei to wall off foreign access to his flagship models in the first government-ordered AI recall — a DC hand reaching into SV's crown jewels just as Anthropic races to IPO.
Beijing forced Zuckerberg to unwind a completed $2B Manus acquisition — the first time a state has clawed back a closed US-China AI deal, and Meta eats the loss.
Ellison's AI capex blew past estimates again and the credit market flinched — a second straight bruise after Oracle's 8.5% plunge earlier this week, the debt bill coming due in public.
Pichai went on offense, suing a Chinese network that turned Gemini into phishing-ware — Google planting a flag on AI-misuse liability rather than wearing it.
Altman quietly scrapped his Abu Dhabi trip as the federal model-access squeeze made Gulf AI deals radioactive — a small retreat, but a retreat.
